properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Weranga to Tara (near Dalby) - fire as at 11:14am Wednesday, 8 July 2026
properties.CallToAction: Avoid Smoke (Hazard Reduction Burn)
properties.WarningText:
Thick smoke is affecting Weranga, Halliford, Kumbarilla, Tara and surrounding areas. This is from Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service hazard reduction burning in the Kumbarilla State Forest.

Smoke could be in the area for days. Protect yourself from the impacts of smoke.

What you should do:

- If the smoke is thick where you are, stay inside and close windows and doors.

- If you need medication to help you breathe keep it close.

- If you are worried about your health call your doctor or 13 HEALTH (13 432 584). If it is an emergency, call Triple Zero (000) now.

- If you are affected by the smoke you may need to leave the area.

- Do not fly drones around the fire as they affect firefighting.

Impacts:

- Smoke could make it hard for some people to breathe and can cause health issues.

- Some roads may be closed or impacted by smoke. It could be hard to see. You may need to drive a different way.

More information:

- For all fire warnings, visit the Qld Fire website.

- Follow Queensland Fire Department on Facebook.

- For road closures, visit the QLD Traffic website or call 13 19 40.

- For local information visit your Council’s Local Disaster Dashboard.

- If you need translation, call TIS National on 131 450.

- For information about camp ground and park closures, visit the Park Alerts website.

Updates will be issued if the situation changes.

This warning is from the Queensland Fire Department.

properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Gunalda - fire as at 11:18am Saturday, 11 July 2026
properties.CallToAction: Avoid Smoke (Hazard Reduction Burn)

properties.WarningTitle: AVOID SMOKE (HAZARD REDUCTION BURN) - Yaamba - fire as at 2:48pm Saturday, 11 July 2026
properties.CallToAction: Avoid Smoke (Hazard Reduction Burn)
properties.WarningText:
Thick smoke is affecting Yaamba and surrounding areas. This is from hazard reduction burning near Iris Street.

Protect yourself from the impacts of smoke.

What you should do:

- If the smoke is thick where you are, stay inside and close windows and doors.

- If you need medication to help you breathe keep it close.

- If you are worried about your health call your doctor or 13 HEALTH (13 432 584). If it is an emergency, call Triple Zero (000) now.

- If you are affected by the smoke you may need to leave the area.

- Do not fly drones around the fire as they affect firefighting.
Impacts:

- Smoke could make it hard for some people to breathe and can cause health issues.

- Some roads may be closed or impacted by smoke. It could be hard to see. You may need to drive a different way.
More information:

- For all fire warnings, visit the Qld Fire website.

- Follow Queensland Fire Department on Facebook.

- For road closures, visit the QLD Traffic website or call 13 19 40.

- For local information visit your Council’s Local Disaster Dashboard.

- If you need translation, call TIS National on 131 450.
Updates will be issued if the situation changes.

This warning is from the Queensland Fire Department.

properties.CallToAction: There is a vegetation fire at this location. Smoke may affect residents and motorists in the area. Residents should close their doors and windows and keep medication close by if suffering from a respiratory condition. Motorists should use caution and drive to conditions. If you believe your property is under threat, you should call Triple Zero (000) immediately.
